heading: National Volunteer Coordination Program
label: 742f–1 National Volunteer Coordination Program
notes: References in TextThe National Wildlife Refuge System Administration Act of 1966, referred to in par. (1)(C), consists of sections 4 and 5 of , , and is classified to sections 668dd and 668ee of this title. For further details, see Short Title note set out under .Pub. L. 89–66980 Stat. 927section 668dd of this titleOct. 15, 1966
CodificationSection was formerly set out as a note under .section 742f of this title

Amendments2011—, substituted “National Volunteer Coordination Program” for “Projects” in section catchline.Pub. L. 111–357, § 3(1)Par. (1). , amended par. (1) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Subject to the availability of appropriations, the Secretary of the Interior shall carry out a project at 2 or more national wildlife refuges or complexes of geographically related refuges in each United States Fish and Wildlife Service region.”Pub. L. 111–357, § 3(2)Par. (2). , amended par. (2) generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Each project shall provide for the employment of a full-time volunteer coordinator for the refuge or complex of geographically related refuges. The volunteer coordinator shall be responsible for recruiting, training, and supervising volunteers. The volunteer coordinator may be responsible for assisting partner organizations in developing projects and programs under cooperative agreements under and coordinating volunteer activities with partner organizations to carry out the projects and programs.”Pub. (4) as (3).Pub. L. 111–357, § 4(b), substituted “for each fiscal year through fiscal year 2014” for “for for each fiscal year through fiscal year 2009”.Pub. (2). , substituted “project” for “pilot project”.Pub. L. 108–327, § 3(2)Par. (3). , substituted “after , and every 3 years thereafter” for “after ” and “projects” for “pilot projects”.Pub. children.label: A
children.text: augment and support the capabilities and efforts of Federal employees to implement resource management, conservation, and public education programs and activities across the National Wildlife Refuge System;

children.label: B
children.text: provide meaningful opportunities for volunteers to support the resource management, conservation, and public education programs and activities of national wildlife refuges or complexes of geographically related national wildlife refuges in each United States Fish and Wildlife Service region; and

children.label: C
children.text: fulfill the purpose and mission of the National Wildlife Refuge System under the National Wildlife Refuge System Administration Act of 1966 ( et seq.).16 U.S.C. 668dd

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: No later than one year after , the Director shall publish in the Federal Register a national strategy for the coordination and utilization of volunteers within the National Wildlife Refuge System.January 4, 2011

children.heading: Consultation required
children.label: B Consultation required
children.text: The strategy shall be developed in consultation with State fish and wildlife agencies, Indian tribes, refuge friends groups or similar volunteer organizations, and other relevant stakeholders.

children.heading: Volunteer coordinators
children.label: C Volunteer coordinators
children.text: The Director shall provide, subject to the availability of appropriations, no less than one regional volunteer coordinator for each United States Fish and Wildlife Service region to implement the strategy published under this paragraph. Such coordinators may be responsible for assisting partner organizations in developing and implementing volunteer projects and activities under cooperative agreements under .section 742f(d) of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 3 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: There is authorized to be appropriated to carry out this subsection $2,000,000 for each fiscal year through fiscal year 2014.
